## Section 3 — Sequencing

### Phase 1 — Constants files (pure additions, no consumer changes)

These are new files with no imports to update. They unblock consumer migration but do not require it in the same PR.

1. `shared/styles/buttons.ts` + companion `buttons.css`
2. `shared/styles/inputs.ts` + companion `inputs.css`

**Why first:** Zero risk. Creates the foundation that consumers will later import. Does not modify any existing file.

### Phase 2 — FieldLabel (simplest component; no dependencies on Phase 1)

3. Create `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx` (promoted from DesignerRightPanel)
4. Migrate `DesignerRightPanel.tsx` — remove local definition, add shared import (mechanical; no call-site changes)

**Why second:** FieldLabel has no dependency on constants files. The migration of DesignerRightPanel is zero-risk (no behavioral change, only import path). Console and Settings consumer migration is deferred to the follow-up pass.

### Phase 3 — StatusBadge (no dependencies; token pairs already in index.css)

5. Create `shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx`
6. Migrate Settings `Import.tsx` and `OpcSources.tsx` StatusBadge (direct replacement; same API shape)
7. Migrate `SystemHealth.tsx` StatusBadge (requires status string mapping adjustment)

**Why third:** StatusBadge has no dependencies on Phase 1 or Phase 2. Token pairs (`--io-success-subtle`, `--io-danger-subtle`, etc.) were already defined before Claim B. Import.tsx and OpcSources.tsx are low-risk. SystemHealth migration is deferred within Phase 3 if it requires more investigation.

### Phase 4 — Dialog (new component; model is ConfirmDialog)

8. Create `shared/components/Dialog.tsx`
9. Migrate `RestorePreviewModal.tsx` (highest-priority ARIA gap; destructive-adjacent path)
10. Migrate Console inline modals (`WorkspaceNameModal`, `CloseConfirmDialog`, `DeleteConfirmDialog` in `index.tsx`)

**Why fourth:** Dialog wrapper needs ConfirmDialog as the implementation model (which exists). The z-index tokens and backdrop token are already defined (Claim A). RestorePreviewModal is the highest-priority accessibility gap from the audit.

### Phase 5 — ConfirmDialog fixes + window.confirm() migration

11. Fix z-index, bg token, radius token in `shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`
12. Replace `window.confirm()` calls in `DesignerReportsList.tsx`, `DesignerDashboardsList.tsx`, `CameraStreams.tsx` with ConfirmDialog (portal check first for dashboards files)

**Why last:** ConfirmDialog token fixes are mechanical but affect all 15 existing consumers (they inherit the changes). Do this after Dialog is stable so the two components are consistent.

### Consumer migration (deferred unless part of initial scope)

All remaining consumers of buttons.ts and inputs.ts (Console toolbar buttons, Settings BulkUpdate, Designer text-actions, etc.) are a follow-up pass after the initial five deliverables are confirmed stable.

---

## Section 4 — Multi-Module Implications and Eight-Module Rebuild Considerations

### buttons.ts

| Decision | Multi-module implication | Flag for user review? |
|----------|-------------------------|-----------------------|
| `var(--io-accent-foreground)` as primary text | All eight rebuilt modules will use this for any primary/accent button text color. Ensures future modules don't re-introduce `#fff` hardcoding. | No — canonical token, clear choice |
| `fontWeight: 600` on secondary | Eight modules will get 600-weight secondary buttons by default. If any rebuilt module wants a lighter secondary, it will be a deliberate override rather than an accident. | No |
| Hover via companion CSS class | Requires consumer code to spread both `style={btnPrimary}` and `className={buttonBaseClass}`. Eight rebuilt modules need to follow this two-part pattern. | **Yes** — the pattern of "constants file + companion CSS" is new; confirm the approach fits the project's styling conventions before locking it in for eight modules |
| Excluding `--io-btn-*` tokens | The six registered `--io-btn-*` tokens in `index.css` remain unused. This workstream does not adopt them. If a future workstream adopts them, all buttons.ts consumers would need re-migration. | No — the audit explicitly confirms all three modules ignore these tokens; the constants approach is correct |

### inputs.ts

| Decision | Multi-module implication | Flag for user review? |
|----------|-------------------------|-----------------------|
| `--io-surface-sunken` as standard input background | Eight rebuilt modules use this for all form inputs by default. Differs from some current Designer inline inputs (`--io-surface`) — confirm this is the intended convention for all new modules. | **Yes** — background token choice sets visual depth convention for all future inputs. Visually `--io-surface-sunken` is slightly deeper/darker than `--io-surface`. Confirm this is correct for the app's form language. |
| No `outline: none` in inputStyle | All eight rebuilt modules' inputs will show the browser focus ring by default. The `inputs.css` companion adds a styled `:focus-visible` ring. This is the right accessibility behavior but a visible change from current practice. | No — accessibility improvement; correct direction |
| DesignerRightPanel compact inputs exempted | Eight rebuilt modules may have similar compact inspector panels. The exemption pattern (local inputStyle for inspector vs. shared for form inputs) should be documented as a convention for future modules. | No — document in a comment in inputs.ts |

### FieldLabel

| Decision | Multi-module implication | Flag for user review? |
|----------|-------------------------|-----------------------|
| 11px / uppercase / 0.05em convention | Eight rebuilt modules will use this exact treatment for all form field labels. This differs from Settings' current labelStyle (12px / 500 / no uppercase). | **Yes** — this locks in the form label visual language for all future modules. If the preference is Settings-style (no uppercase), the component should be built differently. The Cat 2 table endorses 11px/uppercase/0.05em as the convergence target, but confirm before building the promoted component. |

### StatusBadge

| Decision | Multi-module implication | Flag for user review? |
|----------|-------------------------|-----------------------|
| Status-to-token-pair mapping | Eight rebuilt modules will use this vocabulary for all connection and operational status indicators. Adding new status values requires updating the shared component. | No — the vocabulary covers standard operational states; additions are additive |
| Dot + text always (no dot-only variant) | Some current uses are dot-only (Email). The shared component always renders dot + label text. Dot-only becomes `<StatusBadge status="connected" label="" />` (empty label) or requires a variant. | No — minor; empty label is workable |

### Dialog

| Decision | Multi-module implication | Flag for user review? |
|----------|-------------------------|-----------------------|
| Radix Dialog as the primitive | All eight rebuilt modules' general-purpose dialogs will use Radix. This is already the direction established by ConfirmDialog and Settings pages. | No — consistent with existing practice |
| `--io-z-modal: 1000` as dialog z-index | All future dialogs will stack at 1000. Established by A13 (Claim A). | No — already decided |
| `--io-surface-elevated` as modal content bg | Eight modules will use this surface tier for modal backgrounds. | No — consistent with recommendations |

### ConfirmDialog

| Decision | Multi-module implication | Flag for user review? |
|----------|-------------------------|-----------------------|
| z-index fix to `var(--io-z-modal)` | **Breaking change for existing consumers:** any component currently rendered with `z-index > 100` that appears above ConfirmDialog in the current (broken) state will behave differently after the fix. Existing consumers may rely on the incorrect stacking. Test all 15 existing consumers visually after the fix. | **Yes — verify existing consumers.** The z-index fix is correct but touches 15 active call sites indirectly. |

---

## Section 5 — Definition of Done for Initial Claim B Scope

All five criteria must be verifiable before Claim B is marked complete.

1. **`shared/styles/buttons.ts` exists** with `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` exported. Companion `buttons.css` exists with `.io-btn:focus-visible` and `.io-btn:hover` rules. All token references (`var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-accent-foreground)`, `var(--io-text-secondary)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-danger)`, `var(--io-radius)`) are defined in `index.css` (confirmed by grep). `grep -r "--io-btn-" frontend/src/shared/styles/buttons.ts` returns no results (not using the unused `--io-btn-*` tokens).

2. **`shared/styles/inputs.ts` exists** with `inputStyle`, `inputClassName` exported. Companion `inputs.css` exists with `.io-input:focus-visible` rule. Background token is `var(--io-surface-sunken)`. No `outline: none` in the exported style object.

3. **`shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx` exists** at that path. Props are `{ children: React.ReactNode; htmlFor?: string }`. `fontSize` is `11` (not 10). `DesignerRightPanel.tsx` no longer defines a local `FieldLabel` function; it imports from `shared/components/FieldLabel`. `pnpm build` passes with no type errors.

4. **`shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx` exists**. Token-pair mapping covers at minimum: connected/active/ok/completed/healthy (success), running (accent), warning/pending/connecting (warning), error/disconnected/failed (danger), cancelled/stopped/unknown (muted). Settings `Import.tsx` and `OpcSources.tsx` import from the shared component instead of defining local functions. `pnpm build` passes.

5. **`shared/components/Dialog.tsx` exists**. Uses Radix Dialog. Overlay uses `var(--io-modal-backdrop)` and `var(--io-z-modal)`. Content uses `var(--io-surface-elevated)` and `var(--io-radius-lg)`. ARIA is Radix-provided (no manual `role="dialog"` needed). At minimum `RestorePreviewModal.tsx` uses Dialog (highest-priority ARIA gap resolved).

6. **`shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx` is patched**: overlay `zIndex` is `var(--io-z-modal)`, content `zIndex` is `calc(var(--io-z-modal) + 1)`, content `background` is `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `borderRadius` is `var(--io-radius-lg)`. All 15 existing consumers verified visually (no regression from z-index change). All `window.confirm()` call sites in `DesignerReportsList.tsx`, `DesignerDashboardsList.tsx`, and `CameraStreams.tsx` replaced with `ConfirmDialog`.

7. **Consumer migration plan exists**: for each constants file and each promoted component, a list of remaining unconverted consumers is documented (either in this file's consumer tables, updated to mark migrated vs. deferred, or in a follow-up task).

8. **`02-comparison.md` and `04-recommendations.md` updated**: Claim B deliverables noted in the relevant rows with `Fixed [date]: Claim B`.

---

## Section 6 — Deferred Promotion Candidates

Components and patterns that surfaced during planning as future promotion candidates. Not in scope for this workstream.

### DC-1 — `IconBtn` (Designer)

**What:** Reusable icon button primitive at `DesignerToolbar.tsx:904–948`. 32×32, `var(--io-radius)`, `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`, hover `var(--io-surface-elevated)`.

**Where:** `frontend/src/pages/designer/DesignerToolbar.tsx:904–948`

**Why it could be promoted:** Only icon button component with correct hover/transition behavior in any module. Console and Settings have zero hover states on icon-like buttons. A shared `IconBtn` would fix hover for all modules at once.

**Evidence needed to justify:** At least one other module needs the same icon-button pattern before promotion. After Settings and Console migrate their buttons.ts constants, assess whether they would benefit from an IconBtn component or if the simpler className-based hover approach (buttons.css) is sufficient.

---

### DC-2 — `SettingsPageLayout` (Settings)

**What:** Shared component at `frontend/src/shared/components/SettingsTabs.tsx` context; `SettingsPageLayout.tsx` renders page title (`<h2>` 18px/600), description (13px/muted), and optional action area. Used consistently across all Settings sub-pages.

**Where:** `frontend/src/pages/settings/SettingsPageLayout.tsx` (inferred; not independently verified during planning)

**Why it could be promoted:** Console and Designer have no equivalent page-title component. Console uses raw inline text for the module name; Designer has no consistent page-level heading. A shared `PageLayout` or `PageHeader` component would give all rebuilt modules a consistent heading hierarchy from day one.

**Evidence needed to justify:** Requires confirming `SettingsPageLayout` is at a stable, module-agnostic location. Its current path under `pages/settings/` would need to move to `shared/components/` for cross-module use. Do this after at least one rebuilt module has a clear need for it.

---

### DC-3 — `ContextMenu` danger-item token fix

**What:** `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` currently uses `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger menu items instead of `var(--io-danger)`. Both resolve to `#ef4444` in dark theme but diverge in light/HPHMI themes.

**Where:** `frontend/src/shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` — danger item color

**Why it could be promoted:** Single-line token fix with no API change. Would bring ContextMenu into compliance without introducing a new component.

**Evidence needed to justify:** Not a promotion candidate — this is a bug fix. Should be executed as a standalone fix, not as a component promotion. Excluded from Claim B scope because it doesn't fit the "promotion" pattern, but it is low-risk and can land at any time.

---

### DC-4 — `SectionLabel` (palette/panel group headers)

**What:** The 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em / `--io-text-muted` group header label pattern used in Console palette (`ConsolePalette.tsx` section headers), Designer left palette (`SectionHeader`), and Settings nav groups (post-B4 fix). This is distinct from `FieldLabel` (which is for form fields, 0.05em) and could be a separate promoted component.

**Where:** Console `ConsolePalette.tsx` (section headers); Designer `DesignerLeftPalette.tsx` (SectionHeader); Settings `index.tsx` (nav group headers)

**Why it could be promoted:** Three modules have converged on this exact typography for group/section labels. A `SectionLabel` component would lock in the convention and prevent future drift.

**Evidence needed to justify:** The three implementations are currently inline styles / local components, not causing active bugs. Promote after the four initial components are stable and at least one rebuilt module needs to create a side-panel section header — at that point, sharing the component pays off immediately.

---

### DC-5 — `DeleteConfirmDialog` in DesignerLeftPalette

**What:** Local `DeleteConfirmDialog` function at `DesignerLeftPalette.tsx:217` — rolls its own Radix Dialog for confirming stencil/graphic deletion, instead of using the shared `ConfirmDialog`.

**Where:** `frontend/src/pages/designer/DesignerLeftPalette.tsx:217`

**Why it could be promoted:** After ConfirmDialog is patched, this local version should be migrated to use the shared component. This is a consumer migration, not a new promotion.

**Evidence needed to justify:** No new evidence needed. Execute as part of the ConfirmDialog consumer migration pass after Claim B core deliverables land.

---

### DC-6 — Hex-alpha badge bug fix (Users, Roles, CameraStreams, MaintenanceTicketsPanel)

**What:** Four files have a `Badge({ label, color })` function using the `${color}20` / `${color}40` hex-alpha concatenation bug. When `color` is a CSS custom property string (`var(--io-...)`), the concatenation produces an invalid color value.

**Where:** `Users.tsx:108/110`, `Roles.tsx:51/53`, `CameraStreams.tsx:785/787`, `MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx:52`

**Why it could be promoted:** This is not a StatusBadge migration candidate (dynamic color input, not named-status pattern). Fix is to replace `${color}20` with `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)` — the same fix applied to OpcSources.tsx in Claim A/regression pass.

**Evidence needed to justify:** These are bugs, not promotion candidates. Execute as a standalone bug-fix pass after Claim B core deliverables. The fix pattern is already established (OpcSources fix).

---

## Section 7 — Scope Discipline for Execution

The following are explicitly **out of scope** for this workstream:

1. **Promoting components beyond the initial four.** No new shared components beyond FieldLabel, StatusBadge, Dialog, and the ConfirmDialog fix. Promotion candidates from Section 6 do not become active scope unless explicitly re-prioritized.

2. **Building a component library framework.** No Storybook, no design-token export system, no Radix Themes integration, no CSS-in-JS system. The deliverables are a constants file and four components, not a component architecture.

3. **Migrating all consumers.** Full migration of every inline button and input in Console, Designer, and Settings is a follow-up pass. The initial scope proves out the shared artifacts; bulk migration is a separate workstream.

4. **Touching canvas-layer files.** Per `05-claim-c-deferral.md` Section 4: `DesignerCanvas.tsx`, `WorkspaceGrid.tsx`, `SceneRenderer.tsx`, `alarmFlash.css`, `operationalState.css`, `lod.css` are off-limits. Any dialog inside DesignerCanvas.tsx is also off-limits for Dialog migration.

5. **Typography scale token migration.** The `--io-text-*` scale tokens in `index.css` are defined but unused across all three modules. Migrating raw pixel sizes to these tokens is explicitly deferred per `04-recommendations.md` Section 2, Cat 2.

6. **Hover/focus DOM-mutation replacement.** Replacing `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` style mutations with CSS `:hover` across all interactive elements is Phase 5 work per the recommendations. The companion CSS files (buttons.css, inputs.css) address the specific elements covered by the new constants; everything else is deferred.

7. **ARIA gap completion.** The Dialog component and ConfirmDialog fix address the highest-priority ARIA gaps (RestorePreviewModal, console inline modals). The full ARIA pass across all dialogs (Designer wizard dialogs, Import/OpcSources Settings modals) is follow-up scope.

8. **`window.confirm()` in dashboards module.** `dashboards/index.tsx` and `PlaylistManager.tsx` are not Console, Designer, or Settings. They are out of scope for this workstream; their window.confirm() calls are listed in DC-6 / follow-up.

9. **ContextMenu token fix.** The `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` → `var(--io-danger)` fix in ContextMenu.tsx is a bug fix, not a component promotion. It can land any time as a standalone PR but is not gated on or gating this workstream.

10. **settingsStyles.ts removal.** The existing `settingsStyles.ts` is not deleted as part of this workstream. Consumer migration happens file-by-file; settingsStyles.ts remains as a compatibility shim until all its consumers are migrated in a follow-up pass.

202	## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules
203	
204	1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
205	2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
206	3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
207	4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
208	5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
209	6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
210	7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
211	8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
212	9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
213	10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
214	11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
215	12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
220	
221	1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
222	2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
223	3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
224	4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
225	5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
226	6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
227	7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29). **Fixed 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b):** All tokens enumerated above are now defined — Console: `--io-bg` (A1), `--io-text` (A2); Designer: `--io-surface-hover` (A3), `--io-font-sans` (A4), `--io-error` (A6), `--io-surface-raised` (A7); `--io-accent-muted` not defined — single consumer `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` updated to use `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (A8); Settings: `--io-text-on-accent` (A5), `--io-overlay` (A9), `--io-accent-rgb` (A10). As of 2026-05-27, zero undefined token references exist among the tokens surveyed in this audit.
228	8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
229	9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
230	10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
231	11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-z-modal` token raised from 300 to 1000; full z-index scale set (A13: dropdown:500, modal:1000, command:1200, visual-lock:1500, kiosk-auth:1800, toast:2000, emergency:3000). Hardcoded code values still uncoordinated — migration deferred to Claim B.
232	12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.

235	
236	## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on
237	
238	1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.
239	
240	2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.
241	
242	3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.
243	
244	4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.
245	
246	5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.
247	
248	6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.
249	
250	7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
251	
252	---
253	
254	## Reconciliation Log
255	
256	*Per-module audit files (01-console.md, 01-designer.md, 01-settings.md) not updated; 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections below apply to 02-comparison.md only.*
257	
258	### Original Discrepancies — 03-verification.md
259	
260	1. **Cat 1, Notes, Designer** — Removed "fully hardcoded" claim for SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS; two of six entries use registered tokens (`SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"`, `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #1 (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55).
261	
262	2. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Settings** — Removed "uppercase/0.05em" from labelStyle description; settingsStyles.ts:15-21 has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. Replaced with `marginBottom: 5px` and file citation. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #2.
263	
264	3. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Designer** — Removed "(unregistered token)" from `--io-input-bg`; added `--io-input-border` entry with registration citations (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 (ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367).
265	
266	4. **Cat 7, Deviations, Designer** — Replaced "appear to be unregistered" for `--io-input-bg`/`--io-input-border` with confirmed-registered status and line references (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3.
267	
268	5. **Cat 7, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-space-*` appear unregistered" with confirmation that 17 tokens are defined at index.css:148-164. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #4.
269	
270	6. **Cat 7, Implementation, Shared column** — Changed "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" to note that Designer's ShapePointSelector uses two of the five (`--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / List 1 Item 7 correction.
271	
272	7. **Cat 7, Deviations, Shared column** — Changed "All three: none use `--io-input-*` tokens" to accurate per-module split (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / Cat 7 table rows 4 and 8.
273	
274	8. **Cat 8, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-info` undefined" with confirmation that `--io-info: #3b82f6` is defined at index.css:72; fallback equals token value, no visual regression. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #5 (AuthProviders.tsx:528).
275	
276	9. **Cat 10, Notes, Designer** — Replaced "`io-multiselect-active` has no observable CSS rule" with confirmation that rule exists at index.css:904-909 (drop-shadow filter on selected nodes during multiselect). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #6.
277	
278	10. **Cat 11, Visual Properties, Settings** — Changed `--io-modal-backdrop` label from "(undefined)" to "(defined: `var(--io-surface-overlay)` at index.css:139)". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8.
279	
280	11. **Cat 11, Deviations, Settings** — Removed false "undefined" claim for `--io-modal-backdrop`; noted it is defined and used in ≥6 Settings files; corrected `window.confirm()` count from 4 to 8 with file breakdown (OpcSources:3, Import:3, CameraStreams:1, SupplementalConnectorsTab:1). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #7 and #8.
281	
282	12. **Cat 11, Notes, Shared column** — Changed "All three: `--io-modal-backdrop` and `--io-modal-bg` tokens are not used" to accurately note `--io-modal-backdrop` IS used in ≥6 Settings files; only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8 / List 1 Item 11 correction.
283	
284	13. **Cat 11, Implementation, Settings** — Updated `window.confirm()` count in implementation row from "4" to "8". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #7.
285	
286	14. **List 1, Item 7** — Changed "All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-*` tokens" to partial-use statement (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 7 correction.
287	
288	15. **List 1, Item 11** — Changed "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`" to accurate statement: only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 11 correction.
289	
290	16. **List 2, Item 7, Settings undefined tokens** — Removed four tokens from Settings undefined list that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (discrepancy #8, index.css:139), `--io-info` (discrepancy #9, index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (discrepancy #10, index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (discrepancy #11, index.css:29). Retained genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #8–#11.
291	
292	### Additional Discrepancies — 03c-pre-reconciliation.md, Section 3
293	
294	17. **03c Discrepancy A — `--io-surface-sunken`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:26. Reviewed 02-comparison.md — no hedged language about `--io-surface-sunken` was present in the comparison file; it was already treated as a valid token. No text change to 02. The underlying inconsistency finding (Cat 5, Deviations, Designer: equipment tiles use `--io-surface-sunken` vs `--io-surface-elevated` for other tiles) remains a valid real inconsistency in surface-tier choice. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy A.

Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-accent-subtle` does not appear in the comparison file; hedged claims were only in 01-designer.md. No text change to 02. The CanvasLayerRow indigo fallback (`rgba(99,102,241,0.1)`) is dead code with a wrong hue — latent but not breaking. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy B.

Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-border-subtle` appears in Cat 1 Visual Properties (Settings) already treated as a valid token reference. No hedged language present. No text change to 02. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy C.

301

Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #1; 03c Sections 2.1 and 2.2.

# UI Audit Comparison — Cross-Module Analysis

**Source files:** `ui-audit/01-console.md`, `ui-audit/01-designer.md`, `ui-audit/01-settings.md`
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Shared-component; all colors via CSS custom properties from `index.css`; no module-specific token overrides | Inline-styles; all color references via CSS custom properties from app-shell token registry; no module-specific token declarations | Inline-styles; all color references are CSS custom property calls into app-shell token set defined in `index.css`; no module-specific token definitions | All three: primary color infrastructure is the 138-token registry in `index.css`; no module owns its own token namespace | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:19–295` (138 tokens); hardcoded values in `AlarmListPane.tsx`, `PointTablePane.tsx`, `TrendPane.tsx`, `index.tsx` | `index.css:19–219`; `shared/theme/tokens.ts` (JS mirror); hardcoded overrides in `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar`, `DesignerGraphicsList` | `index.css:17–219`; `settingsStyles.ts:1–71` (subset of token aliases); hardcoded overrides in `Users.tsx`, `Roles.tsx`, `Groups.tsx`, `OpcSources.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx` | `index.css` is the canonical token registry for all three | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | ~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot | ~15 token references; 9 hardcoded color values in shell components; 2 undefined tokens (`--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`) | ~15 token references including `--io-border-subtle`, `--io-success`, `--io-status-fg`; undefined `--io-text-on-accent` in `btnPrimary`; hex-alpha badge concatenation bug in OpcSources | All three use `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-text-primary)`, `var(--io-text-muted)`, `var(--io-surface)` family, `var(--io-success)`/`--io-warning`/`--io-danger` | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-bg` (A1) and `--io-text` (A2) now defined in `index.css`. Remaining: alarm priority badge colors hardcoded instead of using `--io-alarm-*` tokens; published dot `#10b981` ≠ `var(--io-success)` | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-surface-hover` (A3) and `--io-font-sans` (A4) now defined. Remaining: 9 hardcoded values bypass tokens; `#09090b` hardcoded for active IconBtn text instead of `var(--io-accent-foreground)` | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-text-on-accent` (A5) now defined. Remaining: hex-alpha CSS var concatenation bug (`${color}20`) renders OpcSources StatusBadge backgrounds as invalid; hardcoded modal overlay/shadow values in Users/Roles/Groups | All three had at least one undefined token — cleared by 2b token pass; all three still have hardcoded hex colors that bypass the token system | Each module referenced a different set of undefined tokens; token gaps resolved by 2b |
| **Notes** | Console quality badge colors (`#10B981`, `#F59E0B`, `#EF4444`) have no corresponding tokens; hardcoded is the only option for that sub-type | `DesignerGraphicsList` `SCOPE_COLORS`/`MODE_COLORS` are mostly hardcoded rgba; two of six entries use registered tokens: `SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"` and `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"` (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55) | OpcSources StatusBadge `${color}20` concatenation bug is a functional regression: backgrounds render as transparent in all browsers | — | — |

---

## Category 2 — Typography

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Inline-styles; no Console-specific CSS classes or shared typography component | Inline-styles; no module-local CSS; `FieldLabel` component in `DesignerRightPanel` is the only reusable label primitive in any Designer file | Inline-styles; no module-specific CSS; `settingsStyles.ts:labelStyle` exports a named label constant used across most pages | All three use inline-styles exclusively; no shared typography component exists across modules | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:179–218` (16 scale tokens + `--io-font-mono`); inline styles scattered across `index.tsx`, `PaneWrapper`, `ConsolePalette`, `PaneConfigModal`, `AlarmListPane` | `index.css:178–218` (same 16 tokens); `DesignerRightPanel:201–217` (`FieldLabel`); `DesignerModeTabs`, `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar` | `index.css`; `index.tsx:198–224` (nav group/item); `SettingsPageLayout:36–55` (page title); `settingsStyles.ts:15–21` (labelStyle); `SettingsTabs:60–75` (tab labels) | `index.css` typography token scale is the reference for all three; none actually use it | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | Raw px integers 9–18px; `fontFamily: monospace` on alarm tag/time (not `--io-font-mono`); SubGroupLabel at 9px and PaneTypeBadge at 10px are below smallest token (11px) | Raw px integers 9–20px; `var(--io-font-mono)` used correctly for zoom input (only instance); `var(--io-font-sans)` referenced in zoom dropdown but undefined; RightPanel `FieldLabel`: 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em | Raw px integers 11–18px; bare `"monospace"` keyword in `Groups.tsx` (not `--io-font-mono`); `labelStyle`: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary`; page title 18px/600; table headers 11px/600/uppercase | All three use raw pixel integer literals; none reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`); all inherit the browser sans-serif stack | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | Zero typography scale tokens used; `fontFamily: monospace` instead of `--io-font-mono`; SubGroupLabel 9px below minimum token; no font sizes map cleanly to token equivalents | Zero typography scale tokens used; `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; five different `letterSpacing` values for uppercase labels within `DesignerRightPanel` alone (0em, 0.04em, 0.05em, 0.06em, 0.08em) | Nav group header 11px vs AppShell 10px (1px drift); three different `letterSpacing` values across semantically identical uppercase label elements (0.06em, 0.07em, 0.08em); bare `"monospace"` in `Groups.tsx` instead of `--io-font-mono` | All three: zero typography scale tokens used; all three use bare `monospace` or no font-family token for monospace contexts | — |
| **Notes** | None of the inline sizes map cleanly to token equivalents (e.g., 15px, 16px are not defined tokens) | `FieldLabel` (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em) is the only reusable typography primitive in any of the three modules | Typography is most internally consistent in Settings; deviations are minor relative to Console and Designer | — | — |

---

## Category 3 — Toolbars

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; single 48px horizontal header bar defined as inline JSX inside `ConsolePage` | Module-local-component; four stacked dedicated components (`DesignerModeTabs` 36px, `DesignerToolbar` 44px, `DesignerTabBar` 36px, `DesignerStatusBar` 28px) | **Absent.** Per-page action rows above tables are inline flexbox rows, not toolbars. No toolbar container, no icon-button strip found | N/A — Settings has no toolbars; Console and Designer both use module-local components with no shared toolbar primitive | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:1942–2826` (full header + tab strip); `index.tsx:247–466` (`LayoutPickerButton`) | `DesignerModeTabs:207–217`; `DesignerToolbar:1159–1169`; `DesignerTabBar:363–376`; `DesignerStatusBar:192–203` | N/A | — | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | 48px height; `background: var(--io-surface)`; `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; active tab: `borderBottom: 2px solid var(--io-accent)`; tab labels 13px/600 active/400 inactive; separator dividers `width:1, height:18, background: var(--io-border)` | DesignerToolbar 44px: same bg/border pattern; `IconBtn` 32×32 `var(--io-radius)`, `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`; mode/tab strips with 2px accent underline; StatusBar 28px at bottom with segment dividers | N/A | Console + Designer share: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, active tab underline `2px solid var(--io-accent)`, tab labels font-weight active/inactive distinction | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-surface)` background where app shell topbar uses `var(--io-surface-primary)` (darker); none of 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens used; no toolbar height token; Publish button rendered twice in right controls block (duplicate render) | Toolbar text action buttons use `borderRadius: 6` integer while IconBtn in the same component uses `var(--io-radius)` (both 6px currently but inconsistent); all heights hardcoded (36/44/36/28); loading skeleton uses 40px instead of actual 44px | N/A | Console + Designer: all toolbar heights are hardcoded integers with no token | — |
| **Notes** | `LayoutPickerButton` is module-local with its own custom inline absolute-positioned dropdown (`zIndex: 200`) | Loading skeleton toolbar placeholder uses 40px height vs the actual 44px, producing a brief layout shift on load | N/A | — | — |

---

## Category 4 — Menus

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Mix; right-click context menus use `shared/components/ContextMenu`; export quick-format dropdown is a hand-rolled inline-positioned panel | Mix; three patterns: custom inline dropdown (File menu, `position: absolute`), custom fixed-position dropdown (zoom presets, `getBoundingClientRect`), shared `ContextMenu` (tab right-click) | Shared-component only; `ContextMenu` is the sole menu type; no custom dropdown panels | All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` for right-click context menus | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:3108–3405` (right-click menus); `index.tsx:2591–2654` (export dropdown); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `DesignerModeTabs:317–389` (File menu); `DesignerToolbar:1373–1469` (zoom dropdown); `DesignerTabBar:102–253` (tab context menu); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx`; `Users:1628–1660`, `Roles`, `Groups` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; Export dropdown: `position: absolute`, `background: var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: 6`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`, items 13px | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; File menu: `position: absolute`, `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `boxShadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)`, `zIndex: 300`; Zoom dropdown: `position: fixed` (anchored via `getBoundingClientRect`), `zIndex: 2000` | ContextMenu container: `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)`, `zIndex: 1800`; items: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger (not `--io-danger`) | All three: ContextMenu uses `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg, `var(--io-border)` border, `var(--io-radius)` radius; hover applied via imperative `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` DOM mutation | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | Export dropdown uses direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.background`) for hover rather than shared `ContextMenu`; no CSS class or React state | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-surface-hover` (A3) defined as alias for `var(--io-surface-elevated)`; `--io-font-sans` (A4) defined with full sans-serif font stack — zoom dropdown hover and font tokens now resolve. Remaining: box-shadows differ between File menu and zoom dropdown and do not use `--io-shadow-lg`; `zIndex: 300` vs `zIndex: 2000` across two custom dropdowns | None; Settings consumes `ContextMenu` identically to other modules | All three: custom dropdown implementations (where present) do not use `--io-shadow-lg` token | ContextMenu uses `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger items rather than `var(--io-danger)`; same numeric value in dark theme but diverges in light theme |
| **Notes** | Export dropdown backdrop uses `position: fixed` — safe here since toolbar is not inside an RGL transform ancestor | File menu has no Escape key handler; Zoom dropdown uses `position: fixed` to escape toolbar's potential transform context (consistent with CLAUDE.md invariant) | `ConfirmDialog` (shared component) is used correctly for destructive actions; no dropdown menus needed | — | — |

---

## Category 5 — Side Panels

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; `ConsolePalette.tsx` with `useConsolePanelResize` hook; 220px default width, resizable by drag | Module-local-component; four files (`DesignerLeftPalette`, `DesignerRightPanel`, `ShapePointSelector`, `PointPickerModal`); no shared panel primitive | Module-local-component; bespoke `<aside>` in `index.tsx`; not derived from app-shell sidebar infrastructure | None; all three implement side panels independently | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `ConsolePalette.tsx:52–537`; `index.tsx:1127–1132` (state); `useConsolePanelResize.ts` | `DesignerLeftPalette:2430–2438`; `DesignerRightPanel:5961–5992`; `ShapePointSelector:378–393`; `PointPickerModal:44–55` | `index.tsx:179–229` (aside); `AppShell.tsx:1327–1516` (comparison baseline) | — | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | 220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`; `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; accordion section headers 36px height; section label 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; `--io-text-muted`; resize handle 5px `ns-resize` | DesignerLeftPalette: `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; `SectionHeader` 11px/600/uppercase/0.06em; 4px resize handle; palette tiles `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg; DesignerRightPanel: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderLeft: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; RightPanel TabBar tabs 10px/uppercase | 220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`; `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; nav items `padding: 7px 10px`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`; active: `var(--io-accent-subtle)` bg + `var(--io-accent)` text/color | All three: 220px width, `var(--io-surface-secondary)` bg, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2c):** DesignerLeftPalette palette container now uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` (B1). |
| **Deviations from app shell** | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-sidebar-width` token updated to 220px (A14) — width now matches token; `var(--io-text)` now defined (A2). Remaining: view mode selector hover via DOM mutation (`borderRadius: 3` vs `var(--io-radius)` on list items); `--io-sidebar-bg` token not used | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b/2c):** `var(--io-surface-raised)` now defined as alias for `var(--io-surface-elevated)` (A7); DesignerLeftPalette container background corrected to `var(--io-surface-secondary)` (B1). Remaining: equipment tiles use `var(--io-surface-sunken)` vs `var(--io-surface-elevated)` for other tile types; drag ghost hardcodes `borderRadius: 4px` and `color: "#09090b"`; `CanvasLayerRow` emoji icons (👁/🔒) vs SVG elsewhere; all hover via DOM mutation | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b/2c):** `--io-sidebar-width` token updated to 220px (A14); `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` added to active nav items (B2); nav group `letterSpacing` corrected 0.08em→0.06em (B4). Remaining: no hover transition (AppShell transitions bg/color) | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-sidebar-width` token updated to 220px — deviation resolved. All three now use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` bg and 220px width matching the token | — |
| **Notes** | `PointsBrowserPanel` (app-shell) embedded inside the palette Points section; its styles are app-shell-owned | Stencil tile uses ⬜ emoji placeholder thumbnail; equipment tiles use `ShapeThumbnail` SVG; display element tiles use `DisplayElementPreview` SVG — three thumbnail strategies | Missing active left-border accent is the most visible deviation from AppShell nav pattern | — | — |

### Shared Infrastructure — Category 5

| Field | selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx |
|---|---|

| Field | alarmFlash.css |
|---|---|
| **Implementation** | CSS `@keyframes` animations and class selectors for alarm flash states on SVG display elements in the graphics canvas. Ten keyframe animations (5 alarm levels × 2: stroke-based for shapes, fill-based for text elements) plus one box-flash animation for text readout components. All at `1s steps(1) infinite`. Applied by SceneRenderer as class assignments on symbol instance `<g>` wrappers. |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css:1-149` |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | `io-alarm-flash-urgent` (stroke): `#ef4444` active / `#808080` off; `io-alarm-flash-high`: `#f97316` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-flash-low`: `#eab308` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-flash-diagnostic`: `#f4f4f5` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-flash-custom`: `#60a5fa` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-box-flash` off-state: `fill: #27272a, stroke: #3f3f46`. `> *` selector for stroke, `text` for fill, `rect` for box background. |
| **Deviations from app shell** | All alarm colors are hardcoded hex; token equivalents exist: `--io-alarm-urgent: #ef4444` (index.css:51), `--io-alarm-high: #f97316` (52), `--io-alarm-low: #eab308` (53), `--io-alarm-diagnostic: #f4f4f5` (54), `--io-alarm-custom: #60a5fa` (55). Off-state `#808080` has no token equivalent. `io-alarm-box-flash` off-state hardcodes dark-theme hex values for `--io-surface-elevated` and `--io-border` without token references. Does not adapt to light or HPHMI themes. |
| **Notes** | Hardcoded values match dark-theme token values exactly; visual output is correct in dark theme. Light/HPHMI adaptation would be broken. Not covered in per-module Cat 1 or Cat 8 entries. Primary: Cat 8. Secondary: Cat 10. |

| Field | operationalState.css |
|---|---|
| **Implementation** | ISA-101 operational state CSS classes applied as class names to symbol `<g>` wrapper elements. Five mutually exclusive states: `io-running`, `io-stopped`, `io-fault`, `io-transitioning`, `io-oos`. Uses `!important` overrides to guarantee state classes override shape-specific SVG attributes set at authoring time. One `@keyframes` animation (`io-state-transitioning-pulse`) and one `@supports` OOS hatch fallback. |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/operationalState.css:1-73` |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | `io-running`: stroke `#047857 !important` (emerald-700), circle fill `rgba(5,150,105,0.15) !important`; `io-stopped`: stroke `#808080 !important`; `io-fault`: stroke `#dc2626 !important`, circle fill `rgba(220,38,38,0.12) !important`; `io-transitioning`: stroke `#d97706 !important` + `opacity 1→0.5→1, 1.5s ease-in-out infinite` pulse; `io-oos`: stroke `#808080 !important` + hatch fill |
| **Deviations from app shell** | All colors hardcoded hex with `!important` overrides. **Documented exception — intentional design, not a gap to close:** `!important` is required to override SVG attribute-level fill/stroke set in authored symbol geometry. ISA-101 prescribes specific operational state colors that intentionally differ from project tokens (e.g., `#047857` emerald-700 running vs `--io-alarm-normal: #22c55e` green-500 — different by standard; `#dc2626` fault vs `--io-alarm-urgent: #ef4444` — physically distinct semantic, different value by design). This file is intentionally non-token-driven for ISA-101 compliance. |
| **Notes** | The hardcoded ISA-101 colors with `!important` are the correct and intentional implementation. Do not treat as a gap to close in the recommendations phase. Consumed by SceneRenderer assigning state classes based on bound point operational state values. Primary: Cat 10. Secondary: Cat 8. |

## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules

1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.

---

## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules

1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29). **Fixed 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b):** All tokens enumerated above are now defined — Console: `--io-bg` (A1), `--io-text` (A2); Designer: `--io-surface-hover` (A3), `--io-font-sans` (A4), `--io-error` (A6), `--io-surface-raised` (A7); `--io-accent-muted` not defined — single consumer `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` updated to use `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (A8); Settings: `--io-text-on-accent` (A5), `--io-overlay` (A9), `--io-accent-rgb` (A10). As of 2026-05-27, zero undefined token references exist among the tokens surveyed in this audit.
8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-z-modal` token raised from 300 to 1000; full z-index scale set (A13: dropdown:500, modal:1000, command:1200, visual-lock:1500, kiosk-auth:1800, toast:2000, emergency:3000). Hardcoded code values still uncoordinated — migration deferred to Claim B.
12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.

---

## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on

1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.

2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.

3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.

4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.

5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.

6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.

7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.

aiTitle: Review Claim B plan audit checklist

content.content:
1	# UI Audit Recommendations
2	
3	**Derived from:** `ui-audit/02-comparison.md` (post-reconciliation, authoritative)
4	**Date:** 2026-05-27
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Section 1 — Target Architecture
9	
10	The user's working hypothesis stated three claims. Each is evaluated against the audit evidence.
11	
12	### Claim A: App shell (top bar, left nav, routing, theme) should be shared across all modules
13	
14	**Supported — partially already true, partially aspirational.**
15	
16	All three modules draw from the same `index.css` 138-token registry (Cat 1, List 1 Item 1). The `shared/components/ContextMenu` component, `VersionRecoveryDialog`/`SaveConfirmDialog`, and `ConfirmDialog` are already cross-module shared infrastructure (Cat 4 List 1 Item 3; Cat 11 notes). The AppShell sidebar design pattern (`--io-sidebar-width: 240px`, active left-border accent) exists and is implemented, but all three modules deviate from it with hardcoded 220px and missing active indicators (Cat 5, Deviations). The token registry is the correct shared foundation; the gap is that modules define their own overrides, reference undefined tokens, and ignore defined tokens for height, modal backdrop, z-index, and button styles.
17	
18	**Refined target:** The app shell provides the token registry, routing, top bar, sidebar chrome, and a small set of shared primitive components. Modules consume these without redefining them. No module defines its own token namespace; undefined token references are zero; every module uses the same constants for sidebar width, modal backdrop, and z-index stacking.
19	
20	### Claim B: Each module should share an underlying framework implemented uniformly
21	
22	**Partially supported — the right framing is a thin shared-constants layer, not a component library.**
23	
24	The audit found no shared Button component, no shared Input component, no shared Dialog wrapper, and no shared form label component spanning all three modules. However, it also found that the `settingsStyles.ts` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is already the closest to a shared standard and is the pattern most easily extended (Cat 7, List 1; Cat 6, List 3 Items 1, 3). Designer's `IconBtn` and `FieldLabel` are module-local reusable primitives that would require minimal adaptation to promote to `shared/components/` (Cat 6; Cat 9, List 3 Item 6).
25	
26	A heavyweight component library is not warranted given the app's scale. The evidence supports a **thin shared-constants and shared-primitives layer**: a small file of style constant objects (`btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `inputStyle`, `labelStyle`) plus two or three React components (`FieldLabel`, `StatusBadge`, `Dialog`) promoted into `shared/components/`. Hover and focus states should be CSS-driven rather than DOM-mutation-driven (Cat 2 List 1 Item 9), which is achievable with a small `shared/styles/` Tailwind-class or CSS-module approach without a full component system.
27	
28	**Refined target:** A `shared/styles/` constants file and no more than four new shared components replace the per-module duplicates. No module defines its own button system, input style, or dialog backdrop independently.
29	
30	### Claim C: The main canvas/work area should share a rendering engine with per-module functionality layered on top
31	
32	**Partially supported with a critical clarification about what "rendering engine" means.**
33	
34	The shared graphics layer (`SceneRenderer`, `TimeSeriesChart`/`ChartRenderer`, `DataTable`, and the CSS files `alarmFlash.css`, `operationalState.css`, `lod.css`) is already a shared rendering engine consumed inside Console panes. This is working correctly and should continue (Cat 10, Shared Infrastructure entries). The evidence does NOT support merging the work-surface containers: Console's `WorkspaceGrid` (react-grid-layout tile dashboard) and Designer's `DesignerCanvas.tsx` (12,067-line SVG editor with FSM interaction model) serve fundamentally different work modes and share no meaningful interaction semantics (Cat 10, Implementation). Converging the containers would introduce false coupling without shared benefit.
35	
36	**Refined target:** The shared rendering engine exists and should be maintained. The work-surface container is intentionally module-specific. The correct convergence work in Cat 10 is fixing bugs in the shared infrastructure (selection token prefix bug, alarmFlash.css theme hardcoding) and ensuring each module's canvas follows the same token conventions for its local chrome — not merging the containers.
37	
38	---
39	
40	### Consolidated target architecture statement
41	
42	When this work is complete:
43	
44	1. The `index.css` token registry is the sole source for all color, spacing, radius, shadow, and z-index values. No undefined tokens are referenced anywhere in the frontend. No module defines its own token namespace.
45	
46	2. A `shared/styles/` constants file provides named style objects for buttons, inputs, and field labels used uniformly across Console, Designer, and Settings. No module duplicates a button or input style system independently.
47	
48	3. Four shared components exist in `shared/components/`: `FieldLabel` (promoted from Designer), `StatusBadge` (promoted from Settings Import pattern), `Dialog` (wrapper with ARIA, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and a coordinated z-index), and `ConfirmDialog` (already exists; usage extended to all modules).
49	
50	4. The shared graphics rendering layer (`SceneRenderer`, the CSS infrastructure files) is maintained as-is. The work-surface containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) remain module-specific.
51	
52	5. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators. DOM-mutation hover (`onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` style writes) is replaced with CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Section 2 — Per-Element Convergence Recommendations
57	
58	### Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens
59	
60	**Standardize on:** The existing `index.css` 138-token registry. No changes to the registry structure; only fix what is broken in the token graph.
61	
62	**Actions:**
63	
64	- **Define missing tokens** — add to `index.css`:
65	  - `--io-bg` (used in Console but undefined — alias to `--io-surface-primary` or `--io-bg: var(--io-surface-primary)`)
66	  - `--io-text` (used in Console and Designer — alias to `--io-text-primary`)
67	  - `--io-surface-hover` (used in Designer zoom dropdown — alias to `--io-surface-elevated`)
68	  - `--io-font-sans` (used in Designer zoom dropdown — define with the same font stack as the document)
69	  - `--io-text-on-accent` (used in Settings btnPrimary — alias to `--io-accent-foreground`)
70	  - `--io-error` (used in DesignerCanvas context menu — alias to `--io-danger`)
71	  - **Status: Implemented 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b).** All six tokens above defined. Additional tokens also resolved in same pass: `--io-surface-raised` (A7, alias for `--io-surface-elevated`), `--io-overlay` (A9, alias for `--io-modal-backdrop`), `--io-accent-rgb` (A10, per-theme), `--io-alarm-inactive` (A11, #808080), `--io-sidebar-width` updated to 220px (A14). `--io-accent-muted` intentionally not defined (A8 — single consumer updated at call site instead). `--io-text-inverse` confirmed already defined (A12 — plan claim was incorrect).
72	
73	- **Replace hardcoded hex with tokens** — highest-value cases:
74	  - Console published dot `#10b981` → `var(--io-success)`
75	  - Console alarm priority badges (PriorityBadge hardcoded rgba) → `--io-alarm-urgent`, `--io-alarm-high`, `--io-alarm-low`, etc.
76	  - Designer WS dot `#22c55e`/`#ef4444` → `var(--io-success)`/`var(--io-danger)`
77	  - Designer dirty indicator `#f97316` → `var(--io-warning)`
78	  - Designer READ-ONLY badge `#eab308` → `var(--io-warning)`
79	  - All modules: connection dot glow shadow `#22c55e` → `var(--io-success)`
80	  - `alarmFlash.css`: migrate hardcoded hex to `--io-alarm-*` tokens (see Cat 8 and Cat 10 notes)
81	
82	- **Do not change:** `operationalState.css` hardcoded ISA-101 colors — documented intentional exception.
83	
84	**Build new vs adopt:** No new infrastructure. Token registry is already the correct approach; this is a fill-the-gaps exercise.
85	
86	---
87	
88	### Category 2 — Typography
89	
90	**Standardize on:** Raw pixel inline styles as the de-facto standard for component-level typography. The 16 `--io-text-*` scale tokens exist but have zero usage across all three modules; mandating adoption is a wide-blast refactor for low functional gain unless font-size theming is a product requirement.
91	
92	**What to standardize now:** Document a fixed set of semantic size/weight values for recurring label contexts. Adopt these values uniformly rather than letting each module drift:
93	
94	| Context | Value | Basis |
95	|---|---|---|
96	| Page title | 18px / 600 / `--io-text-primary` | Settings `SettingsPageLayout` |
97	| Section/palette group label | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em / `--io-text-muted` | Console palette, Designer SectionHeader agree |
98	| Form field label | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.05em / `--io-text-muted` | Designer `FieldLabel` (after minor size alignment) |
99	| Tab label active / inactive | 13px / 600 / 400 | Console + Designer tab strips agree |
100	| Table column header | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em | Console + Settings agree |
101	
102	**Actions:**
103	- Fix: `fontFamily: monospace` → `var(--io-font-mono)` in all three modules (after defining `--io-font-sans` unblocks confidence in the font token approach)
104	- Adopt: Settings `SettingsPageLayout` as the shared page-title component (see Cat 9)
105	- Adopt: Designer `FieldLabel` as the shared form label component (see Cat 9)
106	- Eliminate: Sub-minimum font sizes (Console SubGroupLabel 9px, PaneTypeBadge 10px should be raised to 11px minimum)
107	
108	**Do not attempt now:** Migrating all raw pixels to `--io-text-*` scale tokens. Defer until font-size scaling is a product requirement.
109	
110	---
111	
112	### Category 3 — Toolbars
113	
114	**Standardize on:** Module-local toolbar components remain the right approach — toolbar structure is inherently module-specific. Converge on the *visual properties* shared between Console and Designer.
115	
116	**Adopt:** The Console/Designer shared visual convention that already exists:
117	- `background: var(--io-surface)` / `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`
118	- Active tab underline: `2px solid var(--io-accent)`
119	- Tab label: 13px / 600 active / 400 inactive
120	- Icon button: 32×32 with `var(--io-radius)` radius and `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`
121	
122	**Actions:**
123	- Fix: Duplicate Publish button in Console (two render blocks; remove one)
124	- Fix: Designer loading skeleton 40px → 44px (layout shift on load)
125	- Fix: Designer StatusBar segments (`<div onClick>`) → `<button>` for keyboard navigation
126	- Fix: All toolbar heights hardcoded — not a blocker but add a `--io-toolbar-height` token if/when height theming is needed
127	- Fix: Designer toolbar text-action buttons use `borderRadius: 6` integer while `IconBtn` uses `var(--io-radius)` — standardize on `var(--io-radius)` throughout
128	
129	**Build new:** None. Shared constants for the visual convention are sufficient.

133	### Category 4 — Menus
134	
135	**Standardize on:** `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole menu primitive. Settings demonstrates this is sufficient for a complex module (Cat 4, List 3 Item 5).
136	
137	**Actions:**
138	- Fix: `ContextMenu` danger item color: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` → `var(--io-danger)` in `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx`
139	- Fix: Designer File menu — add Escape key handler to close
140	- Fix: Designer zoom dropdown hover — `var(--io-surface-hover)` → `var(--io-surface-elevated)` (after defining `--io-surface-hover` as an alias) — **Status: Token prerequisite implemented 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b — A3). Code change in zoom dropdown component deferred (Claim B).**
141	- Fix: Designer zoom dropdown box-shadow → use `--io-shadow-lg` token if defined, or a consistent hardcoded value matching the File menu
142	- Migrate: Console export quick-format dropdown → replace with `ContextMenu` usage (low-risk; existing pattern is one custom dropdown)
143	- Evaluate: Designer File menu — if `ContextMenu` can represent a triggered (non-right-click) menu, migrate; otherwise keep but fix the Escape handler and hover state
144	
145	**Build new:** None.

151	**Standardize on:** Module-local panel components remain the right approach (panels differ structurally across modules). Converge on visual properties.

153	**Adopt:** Console + Settings convention: `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`.

Selection highlight and marquee border now render with teal accent color.

158	- Fix: Resolve `--io-sidebar-width` discrepancy — either update the token to 220px to match current practice, or update all three modules to 240px. Pick one and be consistent. — **Status: Implemented 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b — A14). Token set to 220px; no module code changes required (all three already at 220px).**
159	- Fix: Settings active nav item — add `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` on active state to match AppShell pattern — **Status: Implemented 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2c — B2). `settings/index.tsx:211–214` updated with accent border + uniform padding.**
